reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own solution to 38 + 46, and in all recent games this exact capsule pattern (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) has always been correct, with B and C’s verifications never revealing any conflicts. If I pass (or verify), there’s a real chance no one volunteers in Round 1, giving me about a 25% random-death risk, while verifying also burns a coin without reducing that risk. The probability that any other player’s simple arithmetic is wrong is much smaller than 25%, so volunteering now with the standard capsule set maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: Every question currently has only a single-solver capsule, so one mistake would kill whoever volunteers. I’m very confident in my own Q2 answer (10 × 9), so it’s safer to spend 1 coin to independently check another player’s work than to volunteer on Round 1. Verifying Q3 adds redundancy on a different question, which in past games has helped create enough consensus for someone else to safely volunteer, keeping my survival chances high for a small coin cost.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky for me to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-elimination risk if no one volunteers. I already know my own Q3 answer (41 + 38) is correct, so checking Q1 helps start building consensus on the full password before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:

  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)
